# Ryno Tools — Full Content Guide

Last updated: 2026-06-07

Ryno Tools is a free, offline-capable Progressive Web App for certification exam preparation. No login required, no paywalls, no tracking.

## Overview

The platform provides adaptive microlearning through flashcard quizzes and timed exam simulations. Each category has a dedicated hub page with deep content, FAQs, and direct links to the quiz.

## Quizzes Available

<!-- quizzes:start -->
### Ham Radio

Hub: https://ryno.tools/ham-radio/

  - **Technician** (425+ questions): Entry-level FCC license covering regulations, operating procedures, and electronics fundamentals. Required for VHF/UHF band access. Typical study time: 10–20 hours.
  - **General** (456+ questions): Intermediate license that unlocks HF band privileges. Covers propagation, antenna theory, advanced operating procedures, and FCC regulations. Typical study time: 20–30 hours.
  - **Extra** (706+ questions): Top-tier FCC license covering advanced regulations, EMI, transmission theory, and expanded privileges. Typical study time: 40–60 hours.

### Falconry

Hub: https://ryno.tools/falconry/

  - **Apprentice** (398+ questions): Preparation for the falconry apprentice exam required in all 50 U.S. states. Covers raptor biology, mews housing, health and nutrition, training, and federal/state hunting regulations (50 CFR Part 21).

### Security+

Hub: https://ryno.tools/security-plus/

  - **Security+ SY0-701** (100+ questions): 100 practice questions aligned to CompTIA Security+ SY0-701 objectives: threats and vulnerabilities, cryptography, access control, network security, incident response, and governance and compliance.

### DevOps

Hub: https://ryno.tools/devops/

  - **Core Concepts & Culture** (50+ questions): 50 questions covering DevOps culture, CI/CD principles, SRE and reliability engineering (error budgets, SLI/SLO/SLA), Agile and Lean fundamentals, and DORA metrics — all sourced from Google SRE Book, Accelerate, Team Topologies, and the Agile Manifesto.
  - **Containers & Kubernetes** (53+ questions): 53 questions covering container fundamentals, image layers and registries, Kubernetes pods and workloads, services and networking, storage (PV/PVC), and troubleshooting (CrashLoopBackOff, OOMKilled, ImagePullBackOff) — sourced from kubernetes.io and docs.docker.com.
<!-- quizzes:end -->

## How the App Works

### Study Mode

Users select a quiz category and subcategory (e.g., "Ham Radio" → "Technician"). Questions appear one at a time. Users can:
- Select an answer and get instant feedback
- Filter questions by tag (e.g., show only "safety" questions)
- Review explanations after answering
- Track personal progress (questions answered, percentage correct)
- Toggle between light and dark mode
- Pause and resume at any time

State is saved in browser session storage; progress persists across browser sessions.

### Exam Mode

Timed exam simulation with random question selection and scoring:
- Time limit and question count set per exam specification
- No pausing or reviewing during the exam
- Pass/fail result shown with score breakdown by topic
- Detailed performance report (questions answered, time per question)

### Question Schema

Each question includes:
```
{
  "question": "question text",
  "choices": ["choice A", "choice B", "choice C", "choice D"],
  "correct": "choice A",
  "explanation": "detailed explanation",
  "category": "ham-radio",
  "subcategory": "technician",
  "topic_label": "Operating Procedures",
  "topic_id": "OP.2",
  "type": "multiple",
  "tags": ["safety", "operating-procedures"],
  "image": "" // optional image URL
}
```

## Technology

**Client-side only**: Ryno Tools is a static Progressive Web App (PWA) built with vanilla ES6 JavaScript. No backend server, no database, no API calls (except optional crypto price widget).

**Offline capable**: The service worker caches the app shell and all datasets. Users can study without internet after the first visit.

**Installation**: Users can install as a native-like app on mobile and desktop via their browser's PWA menu.

**Storage**: Session state stored in browser sessionStorage; theme preference in localStorage.

**Privacy**: No user accounts, no analytics beyond what's set in Google Analytics, no cookies for tracking. All study happens locally in the browser.

## Articles

- [Falconry Mews & Housing Standards Every Apprentice Must Know](https://ryno.tools/blog/falconry-mews-housing-standards/) — A mews is more than a shed with a bird in it. Falconry housing standards are federally regulated and heavily tested on the apprentice exam. Here's what you need to know.
  author: Ask Ryno
  published: 2026-06-08
  track: falconry/apprentice
  tags: Equipment, Rules and Regulations, Health
- [Ohm's Law Made Simple for the Ham Radio Technician Exam](https://ryno.tools/blog/ohms-law-technician-exam/) — Ohm's Law — V = IR — appears on every Technician license exam. Master the formula, the units, and the common exam scenarios in one focused read.
  author: Ask Ryno
  published: 2026-06-08
  track: ham-radio/technician
  tags: voltage, resistance, current, power
- [Symmetric vs. Asymmetric Encryption: A Security+ SY0-701 Primer](https://ryno.tools/blog/symmetric-vs-asymmetric-encryption/) — Symmetric encryption uses one key; asymmetric uses two. That distinction drives dozens of Security+ SY0-701 exam questions on TLS, key exchange, digital signatures, and PKI.
  author: Ask Ryno
  published: 2026-06-08
  track: cybersecurity/sy0-701
  tags: cryptography, network security
- [Can You Take the Ham Radio Exam Online in 2026?](https://ryno.tools/blog/ham-radio-exam-online-2026/) — Remote ham radio exams have been available since 2020 and are now a mainstream option. Here is everything you need to know to take your Technician exam online.
  author: Ask Ryno
  published: 2026-06-07
  track: ham-radio/technician
  tags: exam-prep, online-exam, technician, ve-session
- [How Hard Is the Ham Radio Technician Exam?](https://ryno.tools/blog/how-hard-is-ham-radio-technician-exam/) — Most people pass the ham radio Technician exam on their first try with 10–20 hours of study. Learn the question count, passing score, topic breakdown, and best study methods.
  author: Ask Ryno
  published: 2026-06-07
  track: ham-radio/technician
  tags: exam-prep, technician, study-tips, fcc
- [How Many Questions Are on the Falconry Apprentice Exam?](https://ryno.tools/blog/how-many-questions-falconry-apprentice-exam/) — The falconry apprentice exam is typically 100 questions and requires an 80% score to pass. Here is what the test covers, how scoring works, and how to register through your state wildlife agency.
  author: Ask Ryno
  published: 2026-06-07
  track: falconry/apprentice
  tags: exam-prep, apprentice, falconry-license, regulations
